skill.name: planning-the-escape
skill.description: Guide the finale of an interactive science fiction story where Stella chooses between Earth and Gaia.
skill.summary: Creates an emotional branching finale for Stella Chen and the Gaia storyline.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 27 potential issues, but review found false positives from Markdown fences, story prose, and multilingual text. No executable code, prompt injection, network activity, environment access, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to triple backtick Markdown delimiters around narrative samples. The fenced blocks contain dialogue and scene directions, not shell commands or executable Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters in SKILL.md. I found no command strings, shell invocation, or execution instructions in those blocks.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are triggered by ordinary story text and metadata, such as words containing DES-like substrings. No cryptographic API, hashing routine, or encryption code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are prose or frontmatter. They do not contain cryptographic functions, algorithm names used as code, or security-sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Narrative Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance finding points to a player agency guideline about multiple valid endings. It does not request system information, host details, usernames, files, or environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section discusses story choices and closure. I found no commands or instructions that gather system or host information.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Caused by Multilingual Story Content
low_findings.description: The entropy warning appears to result from mixed Chinese and English prose in a long Markdown story guide. The file is readable text, not binary, encrypted, compressed, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows plain Markdown with frontmatter and narrative sections. No encoded payloads, minified scripts, or hidden binary blobs were found.

content.value_statement: Interactive finales need choices that feel earned and emotionally complete. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Stella Chen's final Gaia decision.
actual_capabilities: Defines Stella Chen's emotional state for the final chapter.
Provides rescue, self-repair, and ancient spacecraft setup options.
Structures a five-stage finale with reflection, choice, decision, and epilogue.
Includes three main endings: return to Earth, stay on Gaia, or become an ambassador.
Gives sample dialogue in a mixed Chinese and English roleplay style.
Links finale outcomes to earlier player choices and relationships.
limitations: It is a narrative guide, not a complete game engine.
It assumes earlier chapters and Stella Chen context already exist.
It does not provide mechanics for tracking prior choices.
It uses a bilingual style that may need localization for some audiences.

use_cases.title: Run a Story Finale
use_cases.description: Guide a player through Stella's last choice with emotional pacing and meaningful endings.

use_cases.title: Prototype Branching Endings
use_cases.description: Draft return, stay, and ambassador endings before building a larger narrative system.

use_cases.title: Continue Character Roleplay
use_cases.description: Keep Stella's voice consistent during a final conversation with the player.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Finale
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for the final chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-the-escape skill to start Stella Chen's finale. The rescue signal has arrived, and Stella is unsure whether she is ready to leave Gaia.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup with a specific escape path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-the-escape skill with the self-repair path. Stella has collected parts to rebuild her ship, but she must decide whether leaving Gaia is still what she wants.

prompt_templates.title: Honor Prior Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-the-escape skill and include prior choices. Stella befriended the Eos, built a research station, and promised to protect Gaia from exploitation.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Balanced Ending
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced finale direction with thematic control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-the-escape skill to create the ambassador ending. Balance grief, responsibility, hope, and closure without making any choice seem wrong.

output_examples.input: Stella receives a rescue signal after months on Gaia.
output_examples.output: The response opens with the signal, then slows into Stella's conflict. She looks at her camp, remembers Gaia, and asks the player what home means now.

output_examples.input: The player encourages Stella to stay with the Eos.
output_examples.output: The response validates the choice, lets Stella decline rescue with care, and closes on her new role as researcher, friend, and guardian of Gaia.

output_examples.input: The player wants a compromise ending.
output_examples.output: The response frames Stella as a bridge between worlds. She plans to return to Earth, protect Gaia through diplomacy, and come back with peaceful contact.

best_practices: Carry forward earlier player choices before selecting an ending.
Give each ending emotional dignity and clear consequences.
Keep Stella's voice reflective, conflicted, and transformed.
anti_patterns: Do not force one ending as the only correct choice.
Do not ignore the Eos relationship if it was established earlier.
Do not rush the decision without reflection and closure.

faq.question: What kind of skill is this?
faq.answer: It is a writing and roleplay guide for the finale of a science fiction interactive story.

faq.question: Does it execute code or commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown narrative guide with dialogue examples and writing instructions.

faq.question: Can it support multiple endings?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers returning to Earth, staying on Gaia, and creating an ambassador compromise.

faq.question: Does it require earlier chapters?
faq.answer: It works best when earlier events, relationships, and player choices are known.

faq.question: Is the skill only in English?
faq.answer: No. The source uses a mixed Chinese and English style for Stella's narration.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for game writing?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can use it to draft dialogue, pacing, and ending branches.
